findings.evidence: PR line 27 has `(A : Matrix n n ℝ) (ξ : EuclideanSpace ℝ n)`; Mathlib already exposes the general bilinear identity at `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/SesquilinearForm.lean:185-187`, `Matrix.toLinearMap₂'_apply' ... = v ⬝ᵥ (M *ᵥ w)`, and `Matrix.toQuadraticForm'` is defined generally in `Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/QuadraticForm/Basic.lean:1202-1204`.
findings.fix: State it as `lemma toQuadraticForm'_eq_dotProduct [Fintype n] [DecidableEq n] [CommRing R] (A : Matrix n n R) (ξ : n → R) : A.toQuadraticForm' ξ = ξ ⬝ᵥ (A *ᵥ ξ)` and specialize at use sites.
findings.issue: `toQuadraticForm'_eq_dotProduct` is specialized to `Matrix n n ℝ` and `EuclideanSpace ℝ n`, although the equality is the standard coordinate statement for `Matrix.toQuadraticForm'` over any commutative ring.

summary: One helper introduced in the matrix API is a real/EuclideanSpace special case of a standard matrix identity and should be stated at the natural algebraic level.
